phases.label: Voter Registration
phases.description: The first step to participating in democracy. Ensure your name is on the electoral roll.
steps: Check name in Voter List via Voter Service Portal

requirements: Must be a registered voter in any constituency

phases.label: Candidate Nomination
phases.description: Candidates file their papers to contest the election.
steps: Filing of nomination papers with Returning Officer
voterActions: Check candidate affidavits on ECI site

candidateDetails.disqualification: Failure to lodge account of election expenses
documents: Certified copy of electoral roll entry

phases.label: Campaigning
phases.description: Political parties and candidates reach out to voters with their manifestos.
voterActions: Report MCC violations via cVIGIL app

mccRules: No appeal to caste or communal feelings
No use of religious places for election propaganda
candidateDetails.permits: Required for loudspeakers, rallies, and vehicles

phases.label: Polling Day
phases.description: The day you cast your vote using EVMs and VVPATs.
voterActions: Carry Voter ID or approved alternative ID
Check the blue button on EVM

phases.label: Counting & Results
phases.description: Votes are counted and results are declared.
voterActions: Watch live results on ECI website

glossary.definition: Electronic Voting Machine used to record votes.
Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail for verifying EVM votes.
Model Code of Conduct - guidelines for parties and candidates.
None Of The Above - option to reject all candidates.

quickQuestions: What documents do I need to vote?
How to check my name in the voter list?
Can I vote if I am an NRI?

quiz.question: Which form is used for voter registration in India?
quiz.explanation: Form 6 is specifically used for the registration of new voters.

quiz.question: What does VVPAT stand for?
quiz.explanation: VVPAT stands for Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail.

quiz.question: What is the security deposit for a General category candidate in Lok Sabha elections?
quiz.explanation: A General category candidate must deposit ₹25,000, while SC/ST candidates deposit ₹12,500.

quiz.question: How many proposers are required for an independent candidate to file a nomination?
quiz.explanation: Independent candidates require 10 proposers from their constituency, while candidates from recognized parties require only 1.
